title: Add saga orchestrator for order fulfillment
description: Implements a saga pattern to coordinate the multi-step order fulfillment process across payment, inventory, and shipping services.
diff:
diff --git a/sagas/order_saga.py b/sagas/order_saga.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..b4c5d6e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/sagas/order_saga.py
@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+class OrderFulfillmentSaga:
+    def execute(self, order_id: int) -> str:
+        # Step 1: Reserve inventory
+        inventory_reserved = self._reserve_inventory(order_id)
+        if not inventory_reserved:
+            return "failed:inventory"
+
+        # Step 2: Charge payment
+        payment_charged = self._charge_payment(order_id)
+        if not payment_charged:
+            # Compensate: release inventory
+            self._release_inventory(order_id)
+            return "failed:payment"
+
+        # Step 3: Schedule shipping
+        shipping_scheduled = self._schedule_shipping(order_id)
+        if not shipping_scheduled:
+            # BUG: Only reverses shipping, forgets to refund payment!
+            # Inventory is compensated but payment is not.
+            self._release_inventory(order_id)
+            return "failed:shipping"
+
+        return "completed"
comments: quinn: Saga pattern looks correct. Approve.
rachel: Missing compensation step! If shipping fails, you release inventory but don't refund the payment. The customer gets charged but never receives their order.
sam: The compensation chain must be the exact reverse: release_inventory AND refund_payment. This is a critical financial bug.
author-tara: Good catch, I'll add the payment refund in the shipping failure path.
author: tara
